transcript.text: Hello, hello.
Hi.
Are you already ready for your webinar?
Yeah, I got to drop in 15 minutes for the practice pre-webinar stuff, but.
What's it about?
Our identity attack path management maturity model.
Okay.
We just released a tool that allows users to kind of assess their current maturity level in terms of their attack path management program.
So that's one thing we're going to want to showcase.
And I think it's a very useful tool for organizations to see where they can improve, see if they're maybe even advanced or have an advanced program.
It provides like suggestions, things of that nature, but.
Oh, great.
Hope you get a good turnout.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Hope so.
Hope

Well, I've never hosted a webinar, but I don't know, I'm okay presenting in a room full of people, but I feel like once you start presenting and it's a webinar, there's a lot of people, but it's hard because you forget that all of those people are there and it's just a completely different vibe.
It's just you talking in front of a screen.
So it's just a little, I feel like it's a little more nerve wracking.
I see.
I think I'm the opposite.
Oh, okay.
I prefer to just like not see that they're there.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
All right.
I think we might have everybody here, right?
Cool.
All right.
We can get started.
I have shared the agenda with everyone, so you should get it in your inbox via Notion.
But the big updates this week is, so we are moving forward with.
Working on five blogs this week.
These are the five that we are working on.
If you go to our content OS, if you go to the status, you can also see what's in production right now.
So we will be sharing these with you either later today or tomorrow.
They're currently being edited by one of our editors, and as soon as they're ready, we will share them.
Sev is currently reviewing the three that we sent last week.
We did get some feedback on these three, which we have resolved already.
So it was mostly around tone.
Like the tone, I feel like the tone is a little bit stiff, and I think we need to relax it a little bit.
So that's something that we need to work on, just kind of tweaking the writing guidelines.
But other than that, we, yeah, I'm just going to wait for Sev's comments to kind of.
See what else needs to be fixed in the artifacts.
And then I also wanted to show you the images.
So last week we set up an image generation pipeline for the images.
They just generate the featured images for the blog.

So this is – it takes into account your brand guidelines, the content of the article, and then there's a very specific prompt that our designer, Katya, she inputs it into the workflow, and it generates different options.
So we pick one of the options and we add it into the article.
So this is how this one looks like.
For the labs exploit, it looks like this.
And then for lateral movement, it looks like this.
So I'm curious to hear your thoughts.
Like, does this look close to your brand?
Like, if there's anything that you want to change?
Like, feel free to comment on anything and everything.
I'll just share your feedback with Katya so she can tweak the prompt.
Sorry, we're just talking about, like, the design, right?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Yeah, I think it's kind of far off.
Not far off, but it's not on brand for sure.
Okay, okay.
In general, I think we just use much more flat graphics.
Okay.
Instead of, like, 3D isometric shapes.
Rick, I have an example if you made a postman from the website.
Yeah, that totally works.
Oh, right, okay.
Yeah, something like that.
That's much more in line.
Okay, okay.
Yeah.
What other page could you refer to?
think, like, our Bloodhound Enterprise page is pretty up-to-date.
Yeah, Bloodhound Enterprise page is up-to-date.
There's a bunch of graphics there that are more just flatlay that we use as inspiration.
Yeah.
I mean, the shapes are correct.
That's fine.
But just the style.
Okay.
Okay.
So, like, these types of graphics.
Yeah.
I think Jason dropped one in the chat, so I'm not sure if you saw that one.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I just saved it.
Yeah.
Thank you.
This works.
Yeah.
One thing that also might help is on that same Bloodhound Enterprise page, if you scroll down to our demos, you can kind of go through our arcade and see our product UI.
Okay.
That might also be helpful in terms of just the link, because that's literally our UI, so.
Okay.
Okay, okay.
Yeah, yeah, that's helpful.
I think we can definitely update that and change it.
We've had, like the workflow is very good at mimicking these types of shapes and graphics, and it really does follow any type of input that we give it.
The one place where it does struggle a little bit is if you need an exact hex code for the color.
So that's kind of hard for the LLM to understand what it is.
So it's going to get orange, but like the exact same shade of orange might be a little bit tricky.
Yeah, I think that's okay, as long as it's not like highlighter orange, just completely off.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Okay, okay, that sounds good.
Okay.
I don't think we ever sent the brand, like the visual guide.
Did I not?
That might have been my head.
No worries.
Yeah, this is helpful too.
We can go off of this.
Yeah, but if you have anything else, just send it over, and we can use that as well.
Sounds good.
Okay, perfect.
And then beyond the content, I mean, did you have any feedback on the blogs that we delivered last week?
I know not all of you might have had a chance to take a look, but I still want to ask.
Which ones are there?
These are the three that were delivered last week, yeah.
I just got access to them yesterday, so I haven't had a chance to take a look at them.
I will shortly.
Kristen, I'm not sure if you have any feedback.
I haven't been able to read them yet.
And can you remind
Tell how long, are we reviewing, or like what's the goal, are we reviewing every single one, or I can't remember like what the process was.
Yeah, so we will be delivering five per week, and the goal is to, the goal is to get to a point where the reviews don't take a lot of time.
So for these first couple of batches, we would like to get at least like one person to take a look.

So it could be, um, Sev, or it could be you, like it, for us, it's just important that one person reviews it to catch anything related to like product messaging or, um, anything that might, like there are some things that just require nuance, um, and we want to make sure to capture that.

And then we can kind of work that into the guidelines that we have in our process, um, the goal is at some point, like in a couple of weeks to get to a point where you might just be doing like a quick read and you're not.
Not like doing like a whole review.
And coming into this kind of new, how are these blogs generated?
Like it is like the LLM trained on, or if it even is an LLM, is it trained on like SpecterOps messaging and product functions and, you know, positioning, all that kind of stuff?
Or like what's the...
Yeah.
Yeah.
So I did this walkthrough last week.
So shock, feel free to tune out.
But this is what I walked through last week.
So this dashboard right now, it's not functional.
It's just something that we're experimenting with.
So ignore those numbers.
But this is Atlas.
We have a content generation platform that our development team built in-house and like in collaboration with our content, like editorial team.
So each client's dashboard includes these context artifacts.
They include the company context.
So...
Which is available in Notion.
If you go to the docs, you can actually see the company context, the audience personas, and the writing guidelines.
And those are the three, what we call artifacts, that are pulled during the content generation process.
We also have a proofreader checklist.
This includes just style guide, you know, rules around like M dashes, punctuation, capitalization.
But the company context includes like a description of like your services, like the customers, the products.
And that's why we like to get a lot of comments on those three artifacts, because the more accurate and like more specific they are, the better the output is.
We can also customize and add like additional artifacts depending on the client's industry and the use case.
For example, if it turns out that the workflow.
We can't seem to get product messaging right.

This is especially true with platforms that have lots of different products and individual features, and it's hard for the LLM to take all of that context at the beginning and create an output that is accurate.

We can add a step at the end that's kind of like a fact checker just for the product, and we can create an additional artifact with just information about the platform and all the different features, and then it will evaluate the final draft against that artifact and highlight any errors and then go back in and fix them.
So the editorial, like the article generation process, we have like an agentic pipeline, which means that as we are generating content, we are also using AI to grade and evaluate itself.
So it starts with...

We usually give it a title or a keyword, and along with the input, we also provide an assignment direction that includes an outline, who the primary persona is, who's the secondary persona, and this is something that the LLM picks from the artifacts, so it decides who it's going to use as the primary persona.
And then from here, from the outline that we provide in the beginning, it will research the topic.
So we are using EXA for research.

Before, we were using perplexity, but we found that perplexity, especially for more complex industries like healthcare, software development, cybersecurity, it just doesn't, is unable to find high-quality information at the level of EXA.
So...
So...
So this research step, it asks questions about the topic that we provided as the input, and then it synthesizes all of those answers into a research document.
And then it's going to evaluate itself against these factors.

For example, it's checking if the sources are high quality, how old the sources are, if the sources actually, the information pulled from those sources, did it actually answer the question in the initial research document?
And then it will grade the research, and if any of the answers don't meet the research standard, it's going to go back and gather additional information.
If it's unable to find information to back up a specific fact, that's going to be noted in the fact checking at the end.
So for example, if during the article generation process, it says that a specific tool is going to lead to a specific...
The fact checker is going to say, although some industry experts may suggest a double improvement in something, there is no accurate source to support this statement.
So then we know, okay, the fact checker went in and changed this because it could not verify the information.
From the research topic, we draft the article.
The article is based on the outline that we provided.
And from here, we add the internal links.
We also add the source links.
So any third-party stat that we were citing, if we're claiming a specific outcome or if we're, like, claiming or, like, referencing a report, we add those links.
mean, the AI adds those links.
And then it validates.
It's the writing guidelines.
This means that the draft article already pulls the writing guidelines that we have.
However, AI can sometimes struggle with context.
So we give it too much context and it follows some rules, but it ignores others.
And we added this last step so that the AI will evaluate the draft against the writing guidelines.
So, for example, for this draft, the confidence score on the scale of 1 to 0 was 0.9.
So it mostly followed the writing guidelines and it's going to go back in and fix things that don't meet the guidelines.
And this is also when the proofreader kicks in and it's also going to fix any issues around like capitalization, punctuation, and things like that.
And the last step is the fact checker.
So it's going to evaluate all of the, it's going to go in and like find statements that it's presenting.
As facts, and it's going to, like, look at the research document and basically see, okay, does this sentence map to the information that we collected in the research report?
If it's not there, then it's going to fix and, like, paraphrase or remove the information.
Got it.
That is quite thorough.
Yeah, I hope it was helpful.
Like, it's a bit of a, the agentic pipeline is new, so we started working with it, like, a couple of weeks ago, but the output is a lot better.
Previously, we only had, like, one research step, so we weren't, like, doing it, you know, in, like, a self-evaluation way.
But I did want to show you this so you have, like, an idea.
We're not using, like, ChatGPT to, like, this, like, you know, like, randomly.
Yeah, definitely.
It has quite a bit of steps.
I'm impressed, actually.
It's pretty cool.
Yeah, we have, like, a whole development team.
Yeah.
And that, like, we, the content people on our team, like, most of them never worked with developers.
So it's been an interesting synergy of, like, us trying to explain to them, hey, this is what the process of writing an article looks like.
Now we need to figure out a way to actually create a workflow that's going to, like, produce results that are good.
So, yeah, it was a tricky one.
I can actually show you, like, we can inspect the process.
So we can go into Flow Studio.
So this is the fact checker workflow.
And the first step is it's going to split the article into chunks.
And then it's going to extract passages.
So, for example, like, this is one passage.
And it says attackers consistently exploit memory dumping to extract cache credentials.
And then, like, it gives a reason.
And
Why it was extracted.
And then it lists all of these questions that it needs to answer to validate if this is true.
And then it researches them.
And you can kind of see, okay, like result.
For example, for this question, is it accurate that Bloodhound and similar tools can correlate active user sessions with administrative access rights, blah, blah, blah, blah.
Results, yes, it is accurate.
Not to say that this is like perfect and it's correct 100% of the time, but compared to the previous fact checker that we have, like the results are remarkably better.
We have a client in the healthcare space, so they're a supplement brand.
And for them, we really had to customize this workflow significantly to include multiple fact checkers.
We even have a step that annotates.
It's medical information that needs to be reviewed by a doctor.
So it adds like a little label next to the paragraph that has to be fact-checked by a doctor.
So we can like expand this pipeline with additional steps as needed, depending on, you know, the use case and the industry.
Okay, cool.
All right.
So, yes, Sev, I will be on the lookout for your comments.
In the meantime, where is the agenda?
What did I lose it?
So in the meantime, I am going to share the images with our designers so we can make changes to the image generation workflow.
So, and we are going to publish the calibration block as soon as we do, so, or we can stage it actually, and we can publish.
We are doing the WordPress walkthrough later today.
I think Eric is doing it with one of your team members.
I know how to use WordPress, but I've already logged in, but I don't want to draft anything until you show us where everything lives and how everything works.
So I avoid breaking anything.
Okay, and then one last thing that I wanted to discuss with you is the CTA buttons on your blog.

And this is kind of isn't something that like, you know, we need to like, you know, I'm like curious to handle today, but it is something that I like to talk about at the beginning of every sort of client relationship because it impacts, you know, like lead generation and a lot of like those important things that we want to track.
So when I was like looking at.
The blog, so we have a CTA for Book a Demo at the top, but when it comes to like inline CTAs, I haven't like noticed anything here or in the other blogs that I was reviewing.
So I was wondering if you ever had these types of CTAs on the blog or if you're planning to like implement something like that.
Like a CTA to read something else or?
to show you an example, so this is one of our clients, they're a web development agency, and they're using Sanity, and for example, they have different CTAs throughout the content.
Actually, this is a bad example, but let me show you.
So
A different one.
Okay, yeah, so this is an example.
So they have like a CTA for like the web agency for fast-moving AI companies.
And it leads to their industries page, which talks about like AI and ML industries.
So that's something that I was thinking about.
Like, and it can't, it doesn't have to be, like, it doesn't have to link to like a product or a feature page.
Like, it can link to like a case study section on your site or like integrations, any other part.
It's just like, like eye-catching and it allows us to like get more people's attention.
We don't currently, but I don't know.
think that's like a...
it's Like, don't you So Like, know.
Like,
Growth marketing question.
Okay.
I think it's up to them.
Yeah.
What they want to lead to.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
Yeah.
Let me talk to Rick and Helena on how we should go about that.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
That was just, it's one of those things that kind of comes up.
Not right away, but maybe three or four months down the line where people start looking at content performance and they're like, okay, how many, you know, how many people booked a demo?
How many people, you know, like, did we actually get from, you know, the blogs that we've been publishing?
And the more CTAs that we have, like the higher the likelihood that, you know, we can get people to explore other parts of the site.
So I just wanted to throw that out there.
Yeah.
That definitely makes sense.
And I just want to make sure that.
I don't want to unilaterally make that decision without the involvement of Rick and Helena, because I know they primarily manage a lot of the, you know, aspects of the blog.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
Cool.
All right.
So I think those are all of the updates that I had today.
Did you have any other questions?
No, nothing on my end.
I think I'll work with the team to make sure we get those blogs reviewed and feedback incorporated as soon as possible.
But I think beyond that, think good on my end.
Anyone else from the team have anything?
That makes sense.
Okay.
Okay.
Great.
Well, thank you so much for your time.
And yeah, I'll see you next week.
Okay.
Great.
Thank you.
Thank you.
See ya.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content production, review feedback, and align on next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Blog Images:** The new 3D isometric style is off-brand. GrowthX will pivot to SpecterOps' preferred flat graphic style, using the Bloodhound Enterprise page as the primary reference.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=187.0)
  - [**Content Tone:** Initial feedback flagged the writing as "stiff." GrowthX will relax the tone and update writing guidelines to ensure future content is more engaging.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
  - [**Review Process:** SpecterOps will consolidate reviews for the first few blog batches to one person (Sev or Kirsten) to provide focused feedback and refine the process.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [**Inline CTAs:** GrowthX proposed adding inline CTAs to drive traffic to other site pages. Meshach will consult with Rick and Helena before making a decision.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1224.0)

## Topics

### Blog Content & Review Process

  - [**Production Status:**](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=113.0)
      - [5 new blogs are in editing and will be delivered by tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=113.0)
      - [Sev is reviewing the 3 blogs delivered last week.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
  - [**Feedback & Refinement:**](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
      - [**Tone:** Initial feedback noted a "stiff" tone. GrowthX will relax the style and update writing guidelines accordingly.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=147.0)
      - [**Review Process:** To streamline feedback, one person (Sev or Kirsten) will review the first few batches.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
          - [**Rationale:** This focused approach helps refine the content generation pipeline more quickly.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=492.0)
          - [**Goal:** Eventually, reviews will become quick reads as the pipeline matures.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=528.0)
  - [**Content Generation Pipeline (Atlas):**](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=543.0)
      - [Aida detailed the agentic AI pipeline to explain how content is produced and ensure quality.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=543.0)
      - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=566.0)
        1.  [**Context:** The LLM uses SpecterOps' company context, audience personas, and writing guidelines.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)
        2.  [**Research:** Uses EXA (chosen over Perplexity for better quality in complex industries like cybersecurity).](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=750.0)
        3.  [**Self-Evaluation:** The AI grades its own research and re-researches if quality standards are not met.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=793.0)
        4.  [**Drafting:** Generates an article from an outline, adding internal and external links.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=867.0)
        5.  [**Validation:** The AI validates the draft against writing guidelines and proofreads for style/grammar.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=898.0)
        6.  [**Fact-Checking:** Verifies all factual claims against the research document, flagging or removing unverified statements.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=950.0)

### Blog Image Style

  - [**Problem:** The new AI-generated 3D isometric images are off-brand.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=187.0)
  - [**Solution:** GrowthX will pivot to SpecterOps' preferred flat graphic style.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=187.0)
  - [**References:**](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0)
      - [**Primary:** Bloodhound Enterprise page.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=302.0)
      - [**Secondary:** Product UI demos in the "arcade."](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
  - [**Constraint:** The AI struggles with exact hex codes.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=366.0)
      - [**Decision:** This is acceptable as long as colors are not "completely off."](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)

### Inline CTAs

  - [**Proposal:** Add inline CTAs within blog posts to drive traffic to other site pages (e.g., case studies, integrations).](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1224.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Increases lead generation and improves content performance tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1224.0)
  - [**Decision:** Meshach will consult with Rick and Helena before proceeding, as they manage blog strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1391.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1173.0)
      - [Update the image generation workflow to produce flat graphics using the Bloodhound Enterprise page as a reference.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1181.0)
      - [Deliver the 5 new blogs by tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
      - [Participate in the WordPress walkthrough today to learn the publishing process.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
  - [**SpecterOps:**](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1167.0)
      - [Sev: Complete the review of the 3 delivered blogs and provide feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1167.0)
      - [Meshach: Consult with Rick and Helena on the inline CTA proposal.](https://fathom.video/share/QDfDsE_qcH6cs9rFzacWxaxLg3JPV6DS?tab=summary&timestamp=1224.0)

action_items.description: Update writing guidelines for relaxed tone
Share blog images w/ Katya; update workflow to flat style; stage calibration blog
Discuss inline CTAs w/ Rick & Helena; decide placement/destinations
